Class Card

  • java.lang.Object
    • Card
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.lang.Comparable<Card>

    public class Card
extends java.lang.Object
implements java.lang.Comparable<Card>
    Represents an individual card in a standard 52-card deck (no jokers).

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      static int ACE 
      static int CLUBS 
      static int DIAMONDS 
      static int HEARTS 
      static int JACK 
      static int KING 
      static int QUEEN 
      static int SPADES 

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      Card()
      Creates a new Ace of Spades Card.
      Card(Card toCopy)
      Creates a new Card with the same rank and suit as another card.
      Card(int rank, int suit)
      Creates a new Card with specified rank and suit.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      int compareTo(Card other)
      Compares two Cards' ranks.
      boolean equals(Card other)
      Compares two Cards' ranks.
      int getRank()
      Get the rank (number) of this Card.
      int getSuit()
      Get the suit of this Card.
      boolean isFaceCard()
      Check the rank of this Card.
      boolean isNumberCard()
      Check the rank of this Card.
      void setRank(int rank)
      Set the rank (number) of this Card.
      void setSuit(int suit)
      Set the suit of this Card.
      java.lang.String toString()
      Returns a String representation of this Card, in the form "[rank] of [suit]".

    • Constructor Detail

      • Card

        public Card()
        Creates a new Ace of Spades Card.

      • Card

        public Card(int rank,
            int suit)
        Creates a new Card with specified rank and suit. Use the Card class's constants for face cards and suits. If either argument is out of bounds, the object will use the value -1.
        Parameters:
        rank - this Card's rank/number, 2 to 14 or face-card constant
        suit - this Card's suit from class constants (0 to 3)

      • Card

        public Card(Card toCopy)
        Creates a new Card with the same rank and suit as another card.
        Parameters:
        toCopy - Card to create a copy of

    • Method Detail

      • getRank

        public int getRank()
        Get the rank (number) of this Card.
        Returns:
        the rank

      • setRank

        public void setRank(int rank)
        Set the rank (number) of this Card.
        Parameters:
        rank - the new rank

      • getSuit

        public int getSuit()
        Get the suit of this Card. Use the Card class constants to check the suit.
        Returns:
        the suit value as an int

      • setSuit

        public void setSuit(int suit)
        Set the suit of this Card. Use the Card class constants as the argument.
        Parameters:
        suit - the new suit

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString()
        Returns a String representation of this Card, in the form "[rank] of [suit]".
        Overrides:
        toString in class java.lang.Object
        Returns:
        the String

      • equals

        public boolean equals(Card other)
        Compares two Cards' ranks.
        Parameters:
        other - another Card
        Returns:
        true if the two Cards have the same rank, and false otherwise

      • compareTo

        public int compareTo(Card other)
        Compares two Cards' ranks.
        Specified by:
        compareTo in interface java.lang.Comparable<Card>
        Parameters:
        other - another Card
        Returns:
        a negative number if this Card has the lower rank, a positive number if this Card has the higher rank, and 0 if they are equal

      • isFaceCard

        public boolean isFaceCard()
        Check the rank of this Card. Inverse of isNumberCard().
        Returns:
        true if this Card is a Jack, Queen, King, or Ace; false otherwise

      • isNumberCard

        public boolean isNumberCard()
        Check the rank of this Card. Inverse of isFaceCard().
        Returns:
        true if this Card is Two to Ten, false if it is a face card
